input.content:
s
sdlc
Plan
Dashboard Ponder Threads
Build
Features Milestones
Threads
Need a better feedback system for collaborating on ideas
open 3 comments jordan
Actions page loads too slowly on mobile
synthesized 7 comments
SSE reconnect on network drop feels too aggressive
open 1 comment
The ponder list doesn't show session count for new entries
→ ponder 5 comments

Need a better feedback system for collaborating on ideas

open
opened by jordan · Mar 2 at 18:15 · 3 comments
Core element
v1 · original

The current feedback system (feedback.yaml) is a global capture queue — notes have no context, no author, and no persistence. When you hit "Submit to Ponder" it destroys the queue.

What's needed: a lightweight feedback thread where multiple people can contribute. The thread has a core element (a living summary that evolves) and a flat list of comments. An agent can synthesize comments back into the core element. When an idea is big enough, the thread promotes into a full Ponder workspace.

History: v1 Next synthesis → v2
3 comments
J
jordan Mar 2 · 18:15 absorbed into v1
I think that idea is fine but I need the actual feedback experience to improve. When I start a feedback it should become a thread that has a core element that can update, then when other people comment it synthesizes it and adds it to the thread and then you can turn the thread into a ponder experience.
J
jordan Mar 2 · 18:30
Create an html design so I can see what the new feedback page will look like.
ai
agent:facilitator Mar 2 · 18:45
Two open questions before I spec this: (1) Should threads be a standalone nav item or embedded inside Ponder? (2) Can a thread be created with no body — just a title — as a quick-capture mechanism? These affect both the navigation structure and the create-thread flow.
J
Replying as jordan
⌘↵
New thread
Short, scannable. You can always edit later.
This becomes the living summary — the thing synthesis updates over time.

input.content:
---
session: 3
timestamp: 2026-03-02T18:51:00Z
orientation:
  current: "HTML design prototype created for the FeedbackThread UI. Shows: sidebar with Threads nav item, two-pane layout (thread list + thread detail), core element with version history strip, comment thread with agent/human attribution and incorporated badges, compose box, New Thread modal, and V2 action buttons (Synthesize + Promote to Ponder). Design matches existing dark sdlc aesthetic."
  next: "Jordan reviews the design. Confirm nav placement (standalone Threads vs embedded in Ponder) and title-only quick capture. Then spec uat-feedback-threads as a new feature and commit."
  commit: "Design approved → spec uat-feedback-threads feature → /sdlc-ponder-commit"
---

**jordan · Owner**
create an html design so i can see what the need feedback page will look like

---

**Facilitator**

Built the HTML prototype. Here's what the design contains:

## Design walkthrough

### Navigation
Threads is a **standalone nav item** in the sidebar, at the same level as Ponder — directly below it in the Plan section. This answers one of the two open questions from Session 2: threads are not embedded inside Ponder, they have their own top-level presence.

### Two-pane layout
- **Left pane** — thread list with:
  - Thread title (truncated), status badge (open / synthesized / promoted)
  - Comment count + author
  - "New thread" button at top
- **Right pane** — thread detail

### Thread header
Title + status badge + meta line (author, date, comment count). Two action buttons for V2:
- **Synthesize** — triggers agent synthesis run (purple accent)
- **Promote to Ponder** — graduates thread into Ponder workspace (green accent)

### Core element (living body)
Prominent card at the top of the detail view with:
- "Core element" label + pencil icon — makes it clear this is the living part
- Current version indicator (v1 · original)
- Body text — the evolving summary
- **Version strip** at the bottom showing version history chips (v1 active, next synthesis → v2)

⚑ **Decided:** Core element gets a version strip. Current version highlighted, old versions accessible. This directly addresses Dana's trust concern from Session 2 — you can always see what was there before synthesis ran.

### Comment thread
Comments rendered below the core element with:
- Human avatars (initial) vs agent avatars ("ai") in different colors
- **"Absorbed into v1" badge** on incorporated comments (greyed out + dashed border) — visually clear which comments have been synthesized
- Agent comments shown in blue/purple to distinguish them from human comments

⚑ **Decided:** Incorporated comments get visual treatment (reduced opacity + dashed border + "absorbed" badge) not deleted. The history stays visible.

### Compose box
Fixed to the bottom. Shows "Replying as jordan". ⌘↵ shortcut visible.

### New Thread modal
- Title field (required)
- Core element / body field (optional — answers the title-only question: yes, body can be blank)
- Cancel / Create thread

⚑ **Decided (design):** Title-only quick capture is allowed. Core element defaults to empty body-v1.md that the author can fill in later.

## Open questions remaining

? **Confirmed in design:** Threads is a standalone nav item (not embedded in Ponder). Does Jordan want it differently?

? **Confirmed in design:** Title-only thread creation is supported (core element field is optional). OK?

? **Confirm:** Should the Synthesize and Promote buttons be in V1 (even as disabled) or hidden until V2?

## Design file location

- `/tmp/feedback-threads-design.html` — open in browser
- Also captured as `feedback-threads-ui.html` in the ponder scrapbook

**Commit signal still met.** Ready to spec once Jordan confirms nav placement + quick-capture decisions.

todos.activeForm: Creating HTML prototype of FeedbackThread page
todos.content: Create HTML prototype of FeedbackThread page

text:
The design is open in your browser. Here's what you're looking at:

---

## What the design shows

**Layout** — same two-pane pattern as the Ponder page:
- Left: thread list with status badges (open / synthesized / promoted → ponder)
- Right: thread detail

**Core element** — the living part of the thread. It sits at the top of the detail view in its own card with:
- A version indicator (`v1 · original`)
- A version history strip at the bottom — shows `v1` highlighted, hints at `v2` after synthesis
- This directly solves the trust problem from Session 2: you can always see the history

**Comment thread** — below the core element:
- Incorporated comments are visually greyed out + dashed border + "absorbed into v1" badge
- Agent comments shown in blue/purple
- Human comments show initials avatar

**Compose box** — fixed at the bottom, shows "Replying as jordan"

**New Thread modal** — Title (required) + Core element body (optional → title-only capture works)

**V2 buttons in the header** — Synthesize (purple) and Promote to Ponder (green) — shown so you can see where they land

---

Three things to confirm before speccing the feature:

1. **Nav placement** — design shows Threads as a standalone nav item below Ponder. Is that right, or should threads be embedded inside the Ponder page?
2. **Title-only capture** — design makes the body optional. OK?
3. **V2 buttons** — show them disabled in V1, or hide entirely until V2 ships?

- This directly solves the trust problem from Session 2: you can always see the history

1. **Nav placement** — design shows Threads as a standalone nav item below Ponder. Is that right, or should threads be embedded inside the Ponder page?
2. **Title-only capture** — design makes the body optional. OK?
